query: Microsoft Copilot federated connectors general availability MCP Model Context Protocol LSEG HubSpot Notion partners official 2025

mako.raw:
{"query":"Microsoft Copilot federated connectors general availability MCP Model Context Protocol LSEG HubSpot Notion partners official 2025","model":"mako","response_time_ms":2151,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/copilot/connectors/federated-connectors-overview","title":"Federated connectors overview - Microsoft 365 Copilot connectors | Microsoft Learn","content":"Microsoft 365 Copilot supports federated Copilot connectors to enable organizations to connect their data to Copilot by using Model Context Protocol (MCP).
Federated connectors use MCP to access data in real time, so Copilot can retrieve up-to-date information directly from external systems.
Use Model Context Protocol (MCP) to fetch data in real time.
Don't index external data into Microsoft 365.
Partners who want to make a federated connector available in the gallery can submit their remote MCP server to Microsoft for review.
The MCP server associated with the federated connector determines tool availability.
When appropriate, Microsoft 365 Copilot dynamically selects relevant tools to gather the information needed to answer a request."},{"url":"https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/copilot/connectors/submit-federated-connector","title":"Submit a federated connector - Microsoft 365 Copilot connectors | Microsoft Learn","content":"The federated Microsoft 365 Copilot Connector program allows partners to submit a remote Model Context Protocol (MCP) server to Microsoft for inclusion in the connectors gallery for Microsoft 365 Copilot tenants.
Microsoft enables only tools that perform search and fetch operations.
Your organization has signed a legal agreement that permits Microsoft to use your MCP server and its tools within Microsoft 365 Copilot."},{"url":"https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/copilot/connectors/manage-federated-connectors","title":"Manage federated connector availability - Microsoft 365 Copilot connectors | Microsoft Learn","content":"Federated connectors for Microsoft 365 Copilot enable users to access information from external data sources directly within their Copilot experience.
Microsoft provides default federated connectors that use the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to integrate with popular services and tools.
As an administrator, you can use PowerShell to manage the availability of all default federated connectors across your tenant.
Selectively enable specific connectors while keeping the global disable setting active."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/federated-copilot-connectors-mcp-hit-general-now-reads-mike-bowers-yvd2c","title":"Federated Copilot connectors (MCP) hit general availability — Copilot now reads live data instead of a snapshot","content":"Learn more in our Cookie Policy.
The old way: a connector indexed your third-party system — your CRM, a knowledge base, a project tool — and copied a searchable snapshot into Microsoft 365.
Copilot answered from that index.
The new way: federated connectors are built on the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and query the source system live, at the moment you ask.
The first wave includes partners like HubSpot, Notion, Linear, Canva, Moody's, and LSEG."},{"url":"https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/copilot/connectors/set-up-custom-federated-connectors","title":"Set up custom federated connectors - Microsoft 365 Copilot connectors | Microsoft Learn","content":"A custom federated connector starts with a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that exposes read-only tools to safely surface your data.
The MCP server acts as a bridge between Microsoft 365 Copilot and your internal systems.
OAuth 2.0 — Users authenticate with a third-party identity provider. — OAuth app in the source system and Teams Developer Portal registration.
To use this option, your MCP server or API must use Microsoft Entra ID for access control.
On the Custom connector page, under Connect to MCP server, select Add.
For Microsoft Entra SSO, enter the SSO registration ID from the Teams Developer Portal."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Microsoft Copilot federated connectors general availability MCP Model Context Protocol LSEG HubSpot Notion partners official 2025","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/microsoft365copilotblog/federated-copilot-connectors---bringing-real-time-enterprise-data-within-microso/4515993","title":"Federated Copilot connectors - bringing real-time enterprise data within Microsoft 365 Copilot | Microsoft Community Hub","content":"# Federated Copilot connectors - bringing real-time enterprise data within Microsoft 365 Copilot. Microsoft 365 Copilot leverages Copilot connectors with sync capabilities that can further enrich Work IQ and unify enterprise knowledge across hundreds of SaaS systems into a single, coherent enterprise knowledge base in your Microsoft 365 tenant. Extending that vision, we are announcing **general availability of federated Copilot connectors****1**, a new class of Copilot connectors that bring real-time data to Copilot via Model Context Protocol (MCP). The first wave of Microsoft-built federated Copilot connectors includes partners like Canva, HubSpot, Intercom, Linear, LSEG (London Stock Exchange Group), Moody’s, Notion and more. Today, data through these connectors is available in Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat and Researcher. Federated Copilot connectors in Excel to LSEG and Moody’s are coming soon. ### A new model for real-time data access in enterprise. Built on the emerging industry standard Model Context Protocol (MCP), federated connectors bring third-party data into Microsoft 365 Copilot live at query time, enabling users to reason over real-time information without leaving their workflow.","score":0.8548001,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://m365admin.handsontek.net/microsoft-365-copilot-introducing-federated-copilot-connectors","title":"Microsoft 365 Copilot: Introducing Federated Copilot Connectors - M365 Admin","content":"# Microsoft 365 Copilot: Introducing Federated Copilot Connectors. Microsoft 365 Copilot now supports federated Copilot connectors, enabling secure, real-time data access from third-party sources without storing data in Microsoft services. Available worldwide from April to May 2026, admins can manage connectors via the Microsoft 365 admin center, with initial connectors including Canva, HubSpot, and Google Calendar. Microsoft is announcing the **General Availability (GA)** of **federated Copilot connectors** for Microsoft 365 Copilot. Federated Copilot connectors enable users to securely connect Copilot to popular third‑party sources and **retrieve data in real time** using the **Model Context Protocol (MCP)**. + Admins will begin seeing federated Copilot connectors in *Microsoft 365 admin center* starting on **April 20, 2026**. * Microsoft‑published federated Copilot connectors (connector type: **MCP**) will appear under ***Microsoft 365 admin center → Copilot → Connectors*** and will be **enabled by default**. * Users will be able to connect to federated Copilot connectors directly from:.","score":0.854509,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/aguidetothecloud/posts/53-new-updates-in-microsoft-365-copilot-for-may-2026-including-federated-mcp-con/1620085889548291","title":"53 new updates in Microsoft 365 Copilot for May 2026","content":"53 new updates in Microsoft 365 Copilot for May 2026 — including federated MCP connectors (Canva, HubSpot, Linear, LSEG, Moody's, Notion)","score":0.74563974,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://blog-en.topedia.com/2026/03/federated-copilot-connectors-will-reach-general-availability-in-microsoft-365-copilot","title":"Federated Copilot Connectors will reach General Availability in Microsoft 365 Copilot | Topedia Blog","content":"# Federated Copilot Connectors will reach General Availability in Microsoft 365 Copilot. A few weeks ago, I posted that the Researcher agent includes new Federated Connectors, but users with the AI Administrator role cannot update them via the Microsoft 365 admin center. Microsoft has now announced the General Availability (GA) of **federated Copilot connectors** for Microsoft 365 Copilot. With GA, Microsoft is expanding federated Copilot connectors from the Researcher agent to Copilot Chat and Agent Mode in Excel, enabling users to work with external data directly in their Microsoft 365 workflows. * **The user rollout should start at the end of April 2026.** As mentioned in my previous post, these federated connectors are already available in the Researcher agent, as they are enabled by default in the M365 admin center. Microsoft-published federated Copilot connectors (connector type: MCP) will appear under Microsoft 365 admin center > Copilot > Connectors and are **enabled by default**.","score":0.740494,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/max-neo0218_federated-copilot-connectors-bringing-real-time-activity-7459985617381224448-7QJh","title":"Microsoft 365 Copilot Federated Connectors for Real-Time ...","content":"Microsoft announced general availability of federated Copilot connectors, powered by the industry-standard Model Context Protocol (MCP)","score":0.7395922,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.68,"request_id":"7c5cb8c3-5f5e-46c0-ae55-0e6cce1b0ed2"}
